1. PERMITS & REGULATORY COMPLIANCE (UAE/: 1.1 Abu Dhabi Municipality Building Permit current and displayed
1.2 ADOSH Safety Registration and Compliance Certificate
1.3 EAD Environmental Impact Assessment and NOC
1.4 ADWEA Electrical Connection and Grid Integration Approval
1.5 Power Plant Coordination and Interface Permits
1.7 Civil Defense Fire Safety and Emergency Access Approval
2. SITE PREPARATION & TEMPORARY WORKS SA: 2.1 Site survey and soil investigation completed with competent person approval
2.2 Underground utility mapping and clearances verified
2.3 Site perimeter fencing (min 2.4m) with controlled access gates
2.4 Temporary site facilities (offices, welfare, storage) with safety compliance
2.5 Temporary electrical supplies with RCD protection and earthing
2.6 Site drainage and dewatering systems with environmental controls
2.7 Environmental monitoring undertaken and/or stations (dust, noise, water quality) installed
3. EXCAVATION & CIVIL WORKS SAFETY: 3.1 Excavation permit for depths >1.5m with competent person oversight
3.2 Foundation excavations properly shored with engineered support systems
3.3 Cable trench excavations with proper benching/sloping (1.5:1 minimum)
3.4 Excavation atmosphere testing (O2, H2S, LEL) before entry
3.5 Spoil pile management with minimum 2m setback from excavation edge
3.6 Concrete works quality control (slump tests, cube testing, placement)
3.7 Reinforcement steel work audit and certification before pour
3.8 Underground conduit and duct bank installation with proper marking
3.9 Grounding grid installation with soil resistivity testing
4. CONSTRUCTION EQUIPMENT & MOBILE PLANT: 4.1 Mobile cranes with electrical insulation certification for HV work
4.2 Crane operators with valid UAE licenses and HV training certificates
4.3 Daily pre-operation safety checks logged and signed by competent person
4.4 Excavators and earthmoving equipment with ROPS/FOPS protection
4.5 Concrete pumps and mixers with proper positioning and stabilization
4.6 Telehandlers and forklifts with load charts and certified operators
4.7 MEWPs/Boom lifts with fall protection and ground stability assessment
4.8 Equipment exclusion zones marked with rigid barriers (not tape)
4.9 Trained machine banksmen with communication equipment and high-vis
4.10 Equipment maintenance schedules current with service records
5. ACCESS, EGRESS & TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT: 5.1 Vehicle access routes minimum 6m wide for emergency access
5.2 Pedestrian walkways separated from vehicle routes with barriers
5.3 Vehicle speed limits posted (i.e., max 20km/h) and enforced
5.4 Traffic marshals with certified training for vehicle movements
5.5 Site entrance/exit control with security logging system
5.6 Heavy equipment transport routes with clearance verification
5.7 Emergency vehicle access routes kept clear at all times
5.8 Site perimeter security with CCTV and 24/7 monitoring
6. SAFETY SIGNAGE & COMMUNICATION SYSTEM: 6.1 Electrical hazard warning signs in Arabic and English at HV areas
6.2 Mandatory PPE signs posted at all entry points with pictograms
6.3 Emergency assembly point signage clearly visible and illuminated
6.4 Equipment operation exclusion zone markings with reflective materials
6.5 Fire equipment location signs with access route markings
6.6 Two-way radio communication system for supervisors and operators
6.7 Emergency communication hotline to power plant control room
7. HIGH VOLTAGE EQUIPMENT INSTALLATION S: 7.1 Transformer installation with oil spill containment systems
7.2 Circuit breaker installation with SF6 gas handling protocols
7.3 Busbar installation with proper clearances and support structures
7.4 Lightning protection system installation and grounding verification
7.5 Control and protection panel installation with proper ventilation
7.6 Equipment earthing and bonding system continuity verified
8. ELECTRICAL INSTALLATION & CONNECTIONS: 8.1 HV cable installation with proper pulling tensions and bend radius
8.2 Cable terminations and joints executed by certified technicians
8.3 Control wiring installation with proper identification and documentation
8.4 Instrument and protection system wiring with loop testing
8.5 SCADA and communication system installation and testing
8.6 Electrical Safe System of Work (ESSW) procedures implemented
8.7 Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) procedures with visible locks and tags
9. TESTING & COMMISSIONING SAFETY PROCED: 9.1 Pre-energization safety checklist completed and approved
9.2 HV testing equipment calibrated and insulation tested
9.3 Power plant coordination for first energization sequence
9.4 Hi-Pot testing procedures with proper safety distances
9.5 Insulation resistance testing with safety protocols
9.6 Protection relay testing and coordination studies verification
9.7 Emergency shutdown testing with stakeholder coordination
10. ADOSH HEAT STRESS MANAGEMENT (UAE MA: 10.1 Heat Stress Management Program per ADOSH SF implemented
10.2 Heat Index monitoring with hourly readings during summer months
10.3 Work-rest cycles adjusted based on heat index (ADOSH Table)
10.4 Shaded rest areas with cooling systems (min 1 per 25 workers)
10.5 Cold water and electrolyte drinks available within 200m of work
10.6 Heat illness emergency response plan with cooling equipment
10.7 Worker heat stress training with competency records maintained
11. OPERATOR COMPETENCY & TRAINING VERIF: 11.1 Electrical supervisors with valid UAE electrical licenses
11.2 Safety officers with NEBOSH/IOSH certification and UAE recognition
11.3 Equipment operators with manufacturer-specific training records
11.4 First aid personnel with current certification (min 1 per 25 workers)
11.5 Scaffolders with competent person certification and training records
11.6 General construction workers with site-specific safety induction
11.7 Emergency response team training with quarterly drills recorded
12. SPECIALIZED PPE & ELECTRICAL SAFETY: 12.1 Arc flash rated PPE (Cat 2-4) based on hazard analysis
12.2 Electrical safety helmets (Class E) rated for voltage level
12.3 Insulated gloves with leather protectors and periodic testing
12.4 Electrical hazard rated safety boots (EH marking)
12.5 Flame resistant clothing suitable for desert climate
12.6 Full face shields and safety glasses for arc flash protection
12.7 Respiratory protection for SF6 gas exposure
12.8 Fall protection equipment for elevated electrical work
13. EMERGENCY RESPONSE & MEDICAL FACILIT: 13.1 On-site medical facility with electrical burn treatment capability
13.2 Emergency eye wash/shower stations within 100m of HV work areas
13.3 AED units with trained operators (max 3-minute response time)
13.4 Fire suppression systems suitable for electrical fires (CO2/FM-200)
13.5 Emergency communication system with power plant control room
13.6 Ambulance service coordination with local hospitals
13.7 Emergency evacuation procedures with regular drills
13.8 Spill response kits for transformer oil and chemical materials
14. FINAL HANDOVER & DOCUMENTATION: 14.1 Final audit and acceptance testing documentation complete
14.2 As-built drawings and technical documentation delivered
14.3 Operation and maintenance manuals provided with training records
14.4 Safety systems demonstration and operator training completed
14.6 Final regulatory approvals and certificates obtained
